title: Stenoloma alutaceum
intro: Stenoloma alutaceum, also known as Stenoloma or Golden Cliff Fern, is a species of fern native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and reduce oxidative stress. Stenoloma alutaceum contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stenoloma alutaceum are teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to support joint health and immune function.
taxonomy: Stenoloma alutaceum belongs to the family Stenolomataceae, a group of small to medium-sized, epiphytic ferns. It is a species of fern in the genus Stenoloma, which is typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Stenoloma alutaceum places it within the class Polypodiopsida, an order of vascular plants that includes ferns and their allies. This species is further classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, which consists of non-vascular and vascular plants that reproduce via spores. As a member of the kingdom Plantae, Stenoloma alutaceum is a plant that undergoes photosynthesis to produce its own food.
